title: Bridge Essay: Impaired verbal self-monitoring in individuals at high risk of psychosis.
concept: Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.
**Phase context:** Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

**Impaired verbal self-monitoring in individuals at high risk of psychosis.** — Psychol Med (2010). DOI: 10.1017/S0033291709991991. PMID: 19951449. BACKGROUND: Cognitive models suggest that auditory verbal hallucinations arise through defective self-monitoring and the external attribution of inner speech. We used a paradigm that engages verbal self-monitoring (VSM) to examine whether this process is impaired in people experiencing prodromal symptoms, who have a very high risk of developing psychosis.
